project: LMDS V6.0 — Logistics Master Data System
stack: Google Apps Script + HtmlService + Google Sheets

sec_audit.title: Hardcoded Secrets (API Keys, Tokens, Passwords)
sec_audit.description: No hardcoded API keys, tokens, or passwords found in source code. GEMINI_API_KEY correctly stored in PropertiesService with format validation (01_Config.gs:881). SCG_COOKIE migrated from cell B1 to PropertiesService (V6.0.036 fix). GitHub PAT stored in GitHub Secrets only.
sec_audit.fix: No fix required. Continue using PropertiesService for all secrets. Maintain gitleaks workflow (08-gitleaks.yml) as defense-in-depth.

sec_audit.title: Missing AuthZ on Destructive Operations (13/13 required)
sec_audit.evidence:
7/7 core destructive operations missing isAuthorizedUser_() check:
- createPerson_: 06_PersonService.gs:595 - NO isAuthorizedUser_
- mergePersonRecords_: 06_PersonService.gs:747 - NO isAuthorizedUser_
- createPlace_: 07_PlaceService.gs:732 - NO isAuthorizedUser_
- createGeoPoint_: 08_GeoService.gs:227 - NO isAuthorizedUser_
- createDestination_: 09_DestinationService.gs:89 - NO isAuthorizedUser_
- createGlobalAlias: 21_AliasService.gs:106 - NO isAuthorizedUser_
- applySheetProtection_UI: 19_Hardening.gs - NOT FOUND in analyzed files
sec_audit.description: CRITICAL: 7 destructive write operations lack authorization checks. While these functions are primarily called by pipeline code (which runs as script owner), they are globally accessible and could be triggered via custom menus, triggers, or WebApp actions if routing is added. This violates deny-by-default principle (SEC-002).
sec_audit.verification: for func in createPerson_ mergePersonRecords_ createPlace_ createGeoPoint_ createDestination_ createGlobalAlias; do grep -A 8 "^function $func" src/**/*.gs | grep -q 'isAuthorizedUser_' && echo "✅ $func" || echo "❌ $func"; done

sec_audit.title: Cookie Sanitization (RFC 6265 CRLF Injection)
sec_audit.evidence:
sanitizeCookie_() defined in 18_ServiceSCG.gs with:
- CR/LF removal: clean.replace(/[\r\n\x00-\x1f\x7f]/g, '')
- Minimum length check: clean.length < 10 throws error
- Used in setSCGCookie_UI() before PropertiesService storage
- Cookie passed in UrlFetchApp headers (not URL) per SEC-006
sec_audit.description: Cookie sanitization properly implemented. CRLF injection prevented via control character stripping. Cookie stored in PropertiesService (not cell B1) per V6.0.036 migration. RFC 6265 compliance acceptable for SCG cookie format.
sec_audit.fix: No fix required. Current implementation provides adequate protection against CRLF injection attacks.

sec_audit.title: PII Leaks in Logs (Email, Phone, Name)
sec_audit.evidence:
1 PII leak confirmed:
- 06_PersonService.gs:489: logInfo('Phone match but name mismatch: phone=' + queryPhone + '...')
  → Raw Thai phone number (0xxxxxxxxx format) logged to SYS_LOG

Masking functions ARE defined and used in most locations:
- maskEmail_(): 14_Utils.gs - masks email as s***i@domain.com
- md5Hash_(): 14_Utils.gs - MD5 hash for names/phones
- maskReviewerEmail_(): 12_ReviewService.gs:855 - masks reviewer as ***@domain.com
sec_audit.description: One confirmed raw PII leak: Thai phone number logged at PersonService.gs:489 without masking/hashing. Phone numbers are sensitive PII under PDPA Thailand. SYS_LOG sheet accessible to Viewers means PII could be exfiltrated via dashboard export.
sec_audit.fix:
1. Mask phone before logging: const maskedPhone = queryPhone ? queryPhone.substring(0, 3) + '****' : '';
2. Add automated grep check: grep -rnE 'logError\(|logInfo\(' src/**/*.gs | grep -vE 'mask|md5Hash' | grep -E '[0-9]{10}|@'
sec_audit.verification: grep -rnE 'log(Error|Info).*phone=' src/**/*.gs should return 0 after fix

sec_audit.title: Sheet Protection (Unprotected Destructive Ops)
sec_audit.evidence:
applySheetProtection_UI() found in 19_Hardening.gs but grep for protect().setDescription returned incomplete results due to glob pattern. Manual review needed for exact count.

Expected: 8 sheets + Q_REVIEW range protected:
- EMPLOYEE, PERSON, SOURCE, GEO_POINT, PLACE, DESTINATION, ALIAS, FACT_DELIVERY
- Q_REVIEW Decision+Reviewer columns
sec_audit.description: Sheet protection function exists but automated verification inconclusive. Manual verification recommended to confirm all 8 master sheets plus Q_REVIEW decision range are protected. Protection prevents direct cell editing bypassing AuthZ.
sec_audit.fix:
1. Run applySheetProtection_UI() manually from menu after deploy
2. Add unit test verifying protection count >= 9 (8 sheets + 1 range)
3. Consider adding protection check to preflight audit (19_Hardening.gs)
sec_audit.verification: Run applySheetProtection_UI from LMDS menu, then verify protections in Sheet UI

sec_audit.title: API Key in URL vs HTTP Header
sec_audit.description: All external API calls correctly pass credentials in HTTP headers, not URL query strings. This prevents credential leakage in access logs, browser history, referrer headers, and Stackdriver logs.
sec_audit.fix: No fix required. Current implementation follows Google security best practices.

sec_audit.title: Reviewer Email Masking in Audit Trail
sec_audit.evidence:
maskReviewerEmail_() properly used in 4 locations:
- 12_ReviewService.gs:240: reviewer = maskReviewerEmail_(rawEmail)
- 12_ReviewService.gs:456: reviewer = maskReviewerEmail_(rawEmail)
- 12_ReviewService.gs:912: markedBy = maskReviewerEmail_(...)
- 22_WebApp.gs:241: Fallback implementation in maskEmailSafe_()

Format: ***@domain.com (domain visible, local part masked)
sec_audit.description: Reviewer emails consistently masked before storage in Q_REVIEW and SYS_AUDIT_TRAIL. Masking function available globally via 22_WebApp.gs fallback. Team structure partially obscured while maintaining domain accountability.
sec_audit.fix: No fix required. Implementation complete and consistent.

sec_audit.title: OAuth Scope Creep (Least Privilege)
sec_audit.evidence:
Exactly 6 scopes in appsscript.json (reduced from 10 in V5.5.017):
1. spreadsheets - SpreadsheetApp (30 files)
2. userinfo.email - Session.getEmail() (21 calls)
3. script.storage - PropertiesService (19 files)
4. script.container.ui - SpreadsheetApp.getUi() (33 calls)
5. script.scriptapp - ScriptApp triggers (3 files)
6. script.external_request - UrlFetchApp (4 files)

Removed scopes (V5.5.017→V6.0):
- gmail.send, gmail.readonly, gmail.compose, gmail.modify (unused)
sec_audit.description: OAuth scopes follow least privilege principle. All 6 scopes verified in use via grep. No unused scopes detected. Reduction from 10→6 eliminates unnecessary permission surface.
sec_audit.fix: No fix required. Before adding any new scope, verify it's absolutely necessary and document in SECURITY.md §2.

sec_audit.title: Cookie Regex RFC 6265 Compliance
sec_audit.evidence:
sanitizeCookie_() uses character class-based validation:
- Removes: \r, \n, \x00-\x1f, \x7f (control characters)
- Validates minimum length (10 chars)
- Does NOT enforce strict RFC 6265 token format (intentional - SCG cookies vary)

Trade-off: Strict regex would break legitimate SCG cookies with unusual formatting
sec_audit.description: Cookie sanitization prioritizes CRLF injection prevention over strict RFC 6265 format validation. This is acceptable given SCG cookie format variability. Control character stripping addresses the primary attack vector (header injection).
sec_audit.fix: No fix required. If stricter validation needed, add optional RFC 6265 token regex as non-blocking warning.

sec_audit.title: PII Masking Completeness (All Log Paths)
sec_audit.evidence:
Masking coverage assessment:
✅ Covered: Most logError calls use md5Hash_() for names/phones
✅ Covered: Emails masked via maskEmail_()
❌ Gap #1: 06_PersonService.gs:489 - Raw phone in logInfo
❌ Gap #2: 21_AliasService.gs:120 - variantName in logDebug (lower risk - console only)
⚠️ Gap #3: Error messages may contain user input fragments (err.message often includes context)
sec_audit.description: PII masking is ~95% complete with one confirmed production log leak (phone number) and one debug log issue. Error message logging pattern (err.message) could indirectly expose PII if error messages include user input. Recommend defense-in-depth hardening.
sec_audit.fix:
1. Fix immediate: Mask phone at 06_PersonService.gs:489
2. Hardening: Truncate err.message to 100 chars in generic catch blocks
3. Optional: Add PII scanner to CI pipeline

sec_audit.title: Sheet Protection Completeness (8 sheets + Q_REVIEW)
sec_audit.evidence:
applySheetProtection_UI() exists in 19_Hardening.gs with expected 8-sheet list. Automated count inconclusive due to glob pattern matching issues. Requires manual verification or test assertion.

Expected protected sheets:
✓ EMPLOYEE, PERSON, SOURCE, GEO_POINT
✓ PLACE, DESTINATION, ALIAS, FACT_DELIVERY
✓ Q_REVIEW (Decision + Reviewer columns only)
✓ INPUT, SYS_CONFIG (hidden, not protected)
sec_audit.description: Sheet protection code appears complete based on source review, but automated verification failed. This is a VERIFICATION gap, not necessarily a code gap. Protection status should be confirmed against live deployment.
sec_audit.fix:
1. Add test: assert(protectedSheets.length >= 8)
2. Add preflight check: List unprotected sheets in runPreflightAudit output
3. Document protection status in deployment checklist
sec_audit.verification: Manual: Open Sheet → Right-click each sheet → Verify protection indicator

sec_audit.title: Response Body Leak in Logs (fetchWithRetry_)
sec_audit.evidence:
fetchWithRetry_() in 18_ServiceSCG.gs:
- On SUCCESS (HTTP 200): return response.getContentText() ← FULL body returned
- On FAILURE: throw new Error('HTTP ' + code) ← No body leaked ✅
- NO slice(0, 200) truncation found in fetch path

Risk: Success response body (may contain PII from SCG API) returned to caller. If caller logs it, PII leaks.
sec_audit.description: fetchWithRetry_ does NOT truncate success response bodies. While error paths correctly avoid logging bodies (SEC-004 fix), the full success body is returned to callers who MAY log it. Current codebase appears not to log success bodies, but this is a defense-in-depth gap.
sec_audit.fix:
Option A (Recommended): Don't fix - current callers don't log success bodies
Option B (Hardening): Return truncated body: return res.getContentText().slice(0, 5000)
Option C (Audit): Add comment: // CALLER: Do not log returned content without truncation
sec_audit.verification: grep -A 20 'function fetchWithRetry_' | grep -q 'slice\|truncate' → currently fails (no truncation)

new_findings.title: Server-Side Template Injection - User Name Rendered Without Escaping
new_findings.description: The <?= ?> template syntax in Google Apps Script does NOT HTML-escape output. currentUser.name is rendered as raw HTML. If name contains malicious HTML/JS (e.g., from compromised OAuth or data injection), it executes in admin browser context.
new_findings.impact: Stored XSS leading to session theft, account takeover, dashboard defacement. Affects every page load for all users. Cannot be mitigated client-side.
new_findings.fix:
Replace <?= currentUser.name ?> with server-side escaped version:
<?!= HtmlService.createHtmlOutput(currentUser.name || '').getContent() ?>

Or add escapeHtml to template evaluation context.

new_findings.title: Server-Side Template Injection - User Email Rendered Without Escaping
new_findings.description: User email rendered using force-printing <?= ?> which bypasses HTML escaping. Email typically safe but represents defense-in-depth failure. Combined with NEW-001, creates persistent XSS vector in header visible on all pages.
new_findings.impact: XSS execution in header bar on every page load. Enables session hijacking, keystroke logging, data exfiltration in admin context.
new_findings.fix: Use <?!= HtmlService.createHtmlOutput(currentUser.email || '').getContent() ?> or implement server-side escapeHtml utility.

new_findings.title: Template Injection - JSON.stringify Output Injected Into Script Context
new_findings.description: While JSON.stringify escapes </script> tags, the <?= force-printing syntax doesn't guarantee protection against all injection vectors. If objects contain attacker-controlled properties with HTML/script content, this enables script injection within <script> context.
new_findings.impact: Full JavaScript execution in application context. Attacker controlling any object property can achieve arbitrary code execution on page load.
new_findings.fix:
Use JSON.parse with proper escaping:
globalThis.__INITIAL_DATA__ = JSON.parse('<?!= JsonStringify(initialData).replace(/'/g, "\\'") ?>' || 'null');

new_findings.title: DOM XSS - Definition List Values Not Escaped in Detail Panel
new_findings.description: buildDefListHtml_() renders values (shipToAddress, soldToName, driverName, matchReason) directly into innerHTML without escapeHtml_(). Data from Google Sheets could contain malicious HTML if sheet is compromised or data imported from untrusted sources.
new_findings.impact: Stored XSS when viewing delivery details. Attacker who can write to source Sheet or upstream SCG API can inject JS executing in admin browsers.
new_findings.fix: html += '<dd class="text-gray-800 flex-1">' + escapeHtml_(r[1]) + '</dd>'; Apply to both <dt> and <dd> elements.

new_findings.title: DOM XSS - Source Sheet Detail Values Not Escaped
new_findings.description: Identical pattern to NEW-004. Source Sheet view renders raw data from SCG API (names, addresses, remarks) without HTML escaping. External API data increases attack surface compared to internal-only data.
new_findings.impact: Stored XSS via Source Sheet data. Upstream API compromise or proxy MITM could enable JS injection visible to all dashboard users.
new_findings.fix: Apply escapeHtml_(r[1]) to all dd element contents in buildDefListHtml_. Escape dt labels for consistency.

new_findings.title: DOM XSS - Leaflet Popup Content Not Escaped
new_findings.description: matchStatus field from map analytics data injected into Leaflet bindPopup() without escaping. Leaflet renders popup content as HTML, so tags in matchStatus execute when user clicks marker.
new_findings.impact: Stored XSS triggered by clicking map markers. Attackers influencing matchStatus in FACT_DELIVERY can execute JS in victim browsers.
new_findings.fix:
Use L.util.escapeHtml() or ViewHelpers.escapeHtml(): marker.bindPopup('' + p.count + '
Status: ' + escapeHtml(p.matchStatus));

new_findings.title: Missing Authorization Check on createPerson_ (Destructive Write)
new_findings.description: createPerson_() writes person records to M_PERSON master sheet without authorization check. Any user with script access (or via WebApp action if routed) can create person records. Violates SEC-002 deny-by-default requirement.
new_findings.impact: Unauthorized person record creation. Data integrity risk. Potential PII injection if combined with missing input validation.

new_findings.title: Missing Authorization Check on createPlace_ (Destructive Write)
new_findings.description: createPlace_() writes place records to M_PLACE master sheet without authorization check. Place data is critical infrastructure for address matching logic. Unauthorized writes could corrupt geospatial matching.
new_findings.impact: Unauthorized place creation. Matching algorithm corruption risk. Geographic data integrity compromise.
new_findings.fix: Add isAuthorizedUser_() guard at function entry. Same pattern as NEW-007.

new_findings.title: Missing Authorization Check on createGeoPoint_ (Destructive Write)
new_findings.description: createGeoPoint_() writes coordinates to M_GEO_POINT without authZ check. Geo data is foundational for Trinity matching (Person+Place+Geo). Unauthorized geo writes could cause cascading match failures.
new_findings.impact: Unauthorized geo point creation. Match engine accuracy degradation. Location-based feature corruption.
new_findings.fix: Add isAuthorizedUser_() guard at function entry.

new_findings.title: Missing Authorization Check on createDestination_ (Destructive Write)
new_findings.description: createDestination_() creates Destination records linking Person+Place+Geo (Trinity) without authZ check. Destinations represent delivery endpoints - unauthorized creation affects logistics planning and reporting.
new_findings.impact: Unauthorized destination creation. Logistics data corruption. Reporting accuracy impact.
new_findings.fix: Add isAuthorizedUser_() guard at function entry.

new_findings.title: Missing Authorization Check on mergePersonRecords_ (Destructive Modify)
new_findings.description: mergePersonRecords_() performs IRREVERSIBLE status change (MERGED) without authZ check. Merge operations destroy data uniqueness and affect all downstream systems. One of the most dangerous operations in LMDS.
new_findings.impact: Irreversible data modification. Person record merging without audit. Cascade effects on aliases, matches, reviews.
new_findings.fix: Add isAuthorizedUser_() guard PLUS require confirmation dialog. Consider soft-delete pattern instead of status change.

new_findings.title: Formula Injection - User Input Written to Sheets Without Sanitization
new_findings.description: User-provided names, addresses written to Sheets via setValues() without checking for formula injection characters (=, +, -, @). If input starts with '=', Excel/Sheets interprets as formula. Risk moderate because primary data source is SCG API (trusted), but Q_REVIEW manual input is vector.
new_findings.impact: Formula execution on Sheet open/export. Potential data exfiltration via external formula references. Calculation corruption.

new_findings.title: Raw Phone Number (Thai PII) Logged Without Masking
new_findings.evidence:
logInfo(
        'PersonService',
        'Phone match but name mismatch: phone=' +
          queryPhone +  // Raw Thai phone: 0xxxxxxxxx
          ', nameScore=' +
          nameScore +
          ' (threshold=' +
          AI_CONFIG.SCORE_MIN_THRESHOLD +
          ')'
      );
new_findings.description: Thai mobile phone number (format: 0xx-xxx-xxxx) logged to SYS_LOG sheet without hashing/masking. Under Thailand's PDPA, phone numbers are personally identifiable information requiring protection. SYS_LOG accessible to Viewer-role users.
new_findings.impact: PDPA compliance violation. PII exposure to unauthorized viewers. Potential phone number exfiltration via dashboard export.

new_findings.title: DOM XSS - Toast Notification Message Not Escaped
new_findings.description: Toast notification messages built via string concatenation without HTML escaping. showToast_ called from many places with various data sources including API error responses (err.message), which could contain untrusted content.
new_findings.impact: XSS through toast notifications. High surface area vulnerability since toast used globally for success/error feedback.
new_findings.fix:
Import ViewHelpers and apply escaping: return '

' + (globalThis.ViewHelpers ? globalThis.ViewHelpers.escapeHtml(message) : message) + '

';

new_findings.title: DOM XSS - Error Message Rendered Without Escaping
new_findings.description: Error messages from catch blocks rendered into innerHTML without HTML escaping. Error messages may contain user-influenced data from failed API responses or request parameters.
new_findings.impact: Reflected XSS through error message injection. Attackers who can influence error messages (via malformed requests, upstream service responses) can inject JS.

new_findings.title: XFrameOptionsMode.ALLOWALL - Clickjacking Risk
new_findings.description: ALLOWALL disables X-Frame-Options header, allowing WebApp to be embedded in <iframe> on external websites. Enables clickjacking attacks where attacker overlays invisible iframe on legitimate-looking buttons. Documented in SECURITY.md §1 as known risk with mitigations.
new_findings.impact: Clickjacking if attacker embeds LMDS in malicious page. Users tricked into clicking hidden dashboard elements. Mitigated by auth layer but still violates secure-by-default.
new_findings.fix:
Short-term: Accept risk per SECURITY.md §1 documentation (auth layer mitigates).
Long-term: Test DEFAULT mode in GAS sandbox. If works, switch to SAMEORIGIN.
Additional: Implement CSP frame-ancestors directive as backup.

new_findings.title: Auth Debug Logging May Expose Email in Production
new_findings.description: Auth debug logging outputs raw effectiveUser email to SYS_LOG. While this is useful for debugging access issues, it exposes user email addresses in production logs. Lower severity because DASHBOARD_USERS list already contains these emails.
new_findings.impact: Minor PII exposure in logs. Email addresses visible in SYS_LOG to Viewers. Low risk given email already in whitelist config.
new_findings.fix:
Option A: Change to logWarn level (only fires on auth failures)
Option B: Mask email: logInfo('[Auth DEBUG] effectiveUser="' + maskEmail_(email) + '"')
Option C: Gate behind DEV_MODE flag: if (DEV_MODE) logInfo(...)

new_findings.title: RBAC Default-to-Viewer Fallback May Grant Excessive Access
new_findings.description: RBAC defaults unknown users to VIEWER role. This is intentional (deny-by-default for admin ops, allow-by-default for read ops). However, if DASHBOARD_USERS is misconfigured, unexpected users might get VIEWER access to dashboard data.
new_findings.impact: Information disclosure if DASHBOARD_USERS misconfigured. Default VIEWER grants read access to FACT_DELIVERY, Q_REVIEW, dashboard stats. Not critical (read-only) but worth documenting.
new_findings.fix: Document explicitly: 'Default role is VIEWER (read-only). To restrict access, configure DASHBOARD_USERS property.' Consider adding 'access_granted_default' flag that admins must explicitly enable.

security_protocols.name: Secrets Management Protocol
security_protocols.rule: API keys, tokens, passwords ห้าม hardcode ใน .gs ไฟล์ ต้องเก็บใน PropertiesService.getScriptProperties() เท่านั้น ยกเว้นค่าคงที่ (constant) เช่น scope names
security_protocols.implementation:
const apiKey = PropertiesService.getScriptProperties().getProperty('GEMINI_API_KEY');
if (!apiKey) throw new Error('GEMINI_API_KEY not set in ScriptProperties');
security_protocols.verification: grep -rnE 'AIza[A-Za-z0-9_-]{35}|password\s*=\s*[\'\"]' src/ appsscript.json ต้องได้ 0 matches
security_protocols.enforcement: CI gate: .github/workflows/08-gitleaks.yml runs on every PR. Block merge if secrets detected.

security_protocols.name: Authorization Before Destructive Operations
security_protocols.rule: ทุก setValue/setValues ที่เป็น destructive (create/update/delete/merge) ต้องมี isAuthorizedUser_() หรือ requirePermission_() check ก่อน ภายใน 5 บรรทัดแรกของฟังก์ชัน
security_protocols.verification: for func in createPerson_ mergePersonRecords_ createPlace_ createGeoPoint_ createDestination_ createGlobalAlias; do grep -A 5 "^function $func" src/**/*.gs | grep -q 'isAuthorizedUser_' && echo "✅ $func" || echo "❌ $func MISSING AuthZ"; done
security_protocols.enforcement: Pre-deploy checklist (lmds-predeploy-checker skill). Block deploy if any destructive op lacks AuthZ.

security_protocols.name: Input Validation & Formula Injection Prevention
security_protocols.rule: ทุก user input ที่จะเขียนลง Sheet ต้อง sanitize ด้วย sanitizeForSheet_() เพื่อป้องกัน formula injection (นำหน้าด้วย ', +, -, @)
security_protocols.verification: grep -rn 'setValues\|setValue\|appendRow' src/**/*.gs | grep -v 'sanitizeForSheet_' → ต้องได้ 0 matches สำหรับ user input paths
security_protocols.enforcement: Code review checklist item (Law 16). Reject PR that writes user input without sanitization.

security_protocols.name: Output Encoding (XSS Prevention)
security_protocols.rule: ทุก user data ที่ render ใน HTML ต้อง escape ด้วย escapeHtml() ก่อนแทรกเข้าไปใน innerHTML หรือ template ห้ามใช้ <?= กับ user data โดยตรง
security_protocols.verification: grep -rn 'innerHTML.*\+' src/**/*.html | grep -v 'escapeHtml' → ต้องได้ 0 matches สำหรับ user data paths
security_protocols.enforcement: ESLint rule: no-inner-html-without-escape (custom). Code review checklist.

security_protocols.name: PII Protection in Logging
security_protocols.rule: PII (email, phone, name, address) ต้อง mask/hash ก่อน log ห้าม log ค่าดิบของ PII ลง SYS_LOG หรือ Stackdriver
security_protocols.verification: grep -rnE 'log(Error|Info).*\b[0-9]{10}\b|log(Error|Info).*@[\w.]+' src/**/*.gs | grep -v 'mask\|md5Hash' → ต้องได้ 0 matches
security_protocols.enforcement: Automated CI check: grep pattern in 09-sonarcloud.yml or dedicated security lint step.

security_protocols.name: Rate Limiting & Quota Management
security_protocols.rule: ทุก WebApp action และ API endpoint ต้องมี rate limiting ป้องกัน DoS/quota exhaustion. ใช้ per-user rate limiter พร้อม backoff
security_protocols.verification: Load test with Apache Bench or similar: ab -n 200 -c 10 <webapp-url> → ต้องได้ HTTP 429 หลังถึง limit
security_protocols.enforcement: CacheService-based limiter with configurable thresholds. Monitor quota usage in 05-scheduled-health.yml.

security_protocols.name: Audit Trail Integrity
security_protocols.rule: ทุก destructive operation (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE) ต้องบันทึกลง SYS_AUDIT_TRAIL ผ่าน logAuditTrail(). Audit trail ต้องมี changed_by (masked email), timestamp, entity_type, action, old_value, new_value
security_protocols.verification: Query SYS_AUDIT_TRAIL after running pipeline. Verify: every CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE has corresponding audit row. No gaps in changed_at sequence.
security_protocols.enforcement: Unit test: run pipeline with 5 rows → assert SYS_AUDIT_TRAIL count >= 5. cleanupAuditTrail_UI retains 90 days.

security_protocols.name: Cookie & Session Security
security_protocols.rule: Cookie ต้อง sanitize ด้วย sanitizeCookie_() ก่อนเก็บใน PropertiesService ห้ามเก็บใน Sheet cells ต้องส่งผ่าน HTTP header (ไม่ใช่ URL parameter)
security_protocols.verification: grep -rn 'SCG_COOKIE' src/**/*.gs → ต้องไม่พบการเขียนลง cell ตรงๆ ต้องผ่าน PropertiesService เท่านั้น
security_protocols.enforcement: gitleaks pattern for cookie-like strings. Periodic audit of Input sheet for stale cookies.

security_protocols.name: OAuth Scope Review Process
security_protocols.rule: ทุกครั้งที่เพิ่ม scope ใหม่ใน appsscript.json ต้อง: (1) อธิบาย justification ใน PR description (2) Review โดย security auditor (3) Update SECURITY.md §2 scope table (4) Bump APP_VERSION
security_protocols.implementation:
// PR Template - Required section:
## OAuth Scope Change
- **Scope**: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/xxx
- **Justification**: Needed for Y feature in Z file
- **Usage Count**: N files / M calls
- **Security Review**: @auditor-3 approved
security_protocols.verification: python3 -c "import json; scopes=json.load(open('appsscript.json'))['oauthScopes']; print(f'{len(scopes)} scopes'); [print(s) for s in scopes]" → ตรวจสอบว่าทุก scope มี justification ใน SECURITY.md
security_protocols.enforcement: PR template enforces documentation. CODEOWNERS requires security team approval for appsscript.json changes.

security_protocols.name: Incident Response Procedure
security_protocols.rule: เมื่อพบช่องโหว่ด้านความปลอดภัย: (1) แจ้งทีมผ่าน Slack/#security (2) สร้าง GitHub Issue ป้ายกำกับ 'security' (3) Patch ภายใน 24-48h สำหรับ P0/P1 (4) Update CHANGELOG.md (5) Post-mortem ภายใน 5 วัน
security_protocols.implementation:
// Incident Response Checklist (docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md)
1. CONTAIN: Revoke sessions, rotate secrets if compromised
2. ERADICATE: Fix vulnerability, push hotfix
3. RECOVER: Restore from backup if data corrupted
4. POST-MORTEM: What happened? Root cause? Prevention?
5. UPDATE: SECURITY.md review history table
security_protocols.verification: Tabletop exercise quarterly. Simulate: P0 XSS found in production → measure time to patch deployment
security_protocols.enforcement: SLA: P0=24hr, P1=48hr, P2=next-sprint. Security issues block release until fixed or risk-accepted by tech lead.

not_yet_checked: Secrets ใน PropertiesService (ตรวจได้แค่ว่าโค้ดอ้างถูก — ไม่รู้ว่าตั้งค่าจริงหรือยัง) - ต้องตรวจสอบ production deployment
Live penetration test (ต้องรันจริง) - ต้องมี staging environment สำหรับ pen-test
Google Sheet sharing permissions (ดูจาก Sheet จริงไม่ได้) - ต้องตรวจสอบ production Sheet ACL
Telegram bot token security (ต้องดู bot config จริง) - ตรวจสอบว่า token ถูก rotate บ้างหรือยัง
OAuth consent screen configuration (ต้องดู GCP project จริง) - ตรวจสอบ consent screen settings
Apps Script API quotas จริง (ต้อง monitor production) - ตั้ง alert สำหรับ quota ใกล้เกิน
CSRF token implementation (GAS same-origin policy mitigates แต่ไม่มี explicit token) - พิจารณาเพิ่ม CSRF nonce
Content-Security-Policy header (GAS WebApp จำกัด CSP control) - ตรวจสอบว่าสามารถเพิ่ม CSP meta tag ได้หรือไม่

summary.risk_justification: 2 P0 XSS vulnerabilities (template injection in Index.html) affect ALL users on EVERY page load. 5 P1 missing AuthZ checks on destructive write operations. While GAS sandbox and execution model provide some mitigation (pipeline runs as owner), the code violates deny-by-default principle and would fail a security review for production deployment with external users.
go_conditions: PATCH IMMEDIATE (Before production with ANYONE/DOMAIN access):
1. Fix P0 XSS: Replace <?= currentUser.name/email ?> with escaped output in Index.html:74-75
2. Fix P1 AuthZ: Add isAuthorizedUser_() to all 7 destructive operations (SEC-002)
3. Fix P1 DOM XSS: Add escapeHtml_() to buildDefListHtml_() in FactDelivery.html:329, SourceSheet.html:372
4. Fix P2 PII: Mask phone number at 06_PersonService.gs:489
- fetchWithRetry_ success body not truncated (callers don't log it)
- Sheet protection count verification gap (manual confirm)
- Set up quota alerts in GCP console
